title: Retire `MLF.Binding.Validation`’s local binding-root query helpers
problem: Verified overlap: `src/MLF/Binding/Validation.hs` still defines local `lookupBindParent`, `isBindingRoot`, and `bindingRoots`, while `src/MLF/Binding/Queries.hs` already owns the thesis-facing binding-tree query API and `src/MLF/Binding/Tree.hs` re-exports it. Keeping a second private copy in validation creates another place for binding-tree read semantics to drift, but trying to eliminate `MLF.Constraint.NodeAccess.lookupBindParent` in the same round would be too broad because that generic query surface is used across presolution, elaboration, and solved-view code and already lines up with separate open read-query relocation work in `TODO.md`.
why_this_is_simpler: This round stays narrowly mechanical: delete the three local helpers from `MLF.Binding.Validation`, import the existing `MLF.Binding.Queries` definitions, and add one direct source guard proving validation no longer defines its own copies. That gives one clear owner for validation’s binding-root reads without touching the much larger `NodeAccess` call-site graph.
why_thesis_exactness_should_be_preserved: These helpers are read-only projections over `Constraint.cBindParents` and `allNodeRefs`; they do not implement extra inference logic beyond the thesis’s binding-tree facts that non-root nodes have one binding parent and roots are exactly nodes without one. Reusing the already-shared `MLF.Binding.Queries` definitions preserves the same binding-tree semantics that `MLF.Binding.Tree` exposes today, so the cleanup is representational rather than semantic.
expected_benefits: Makes `MLF.Binding.Queries` the single thesis-facing owner for binding-root queries consumed by validation.
Reduces drift risk between `checkBindingTree` internals and the exported `MLF.Binding.Tree` query surface.
Keeps the round implementation-sized by explicitly not folding the broader `MLF.Constraint.NodeAccess` migration into it.
Leaves a cleaner ownership boundary for later read-query relocation work.
risks: The round could sprawl if it starts rewriting `MLF.Constraint.NodeAccess` importers instead of stopping at validation.
Unqualified imports in `MLF.Binding.Validation` could create avoidable name ambiguity if the old locals are not removed cleanly.
Without a source guard, future edits could silently reintroduce the duplicate helper block.
required_validation: Add a source guard in `test/BindingSpec.hs` (or an adjacent binding spec) asserting `MLF.Binding.Validation` no longer contains local `lookupBindParent ::`, `isBindingRoot ::`, or `bindingRoots ::`, while `MLF.Binding.Queries` still does.
Run the binding-tree-focused suite: `cabal test mlf2-test --test-show-details=direct --test-options='--match "MLF.Binding.Tree"'`.
Run the full verification gate before closing the round: `cabal build all && cabal test`.
